review_comment: for some older versions of openwrt, for example ntrepid8/openwrt-15.05-x86, guest detection fails since they don't have `/etc/os-release`. [OpenWrt docs](https://openwrt.org/docs/guide-user/installation/after.installation#what_version_of_openwrt_do_i_have_installed) suggests checking the version using a `cat /etc/banner` . This also seems to not work on older versions, but `uname -a` might be a good fallback. Or, maybe older versions of openwrt don't matter too much?
Whoa! 🙇 Thanks for that info. Some of the other guest plugins check a few different files to get the version, so I can do something similar here.
